channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Home theater fans will be happy the LG G6 OLED TV fixes this overlooked picture issue its predecessors had — but there's a catch
item.description: The LG G6 fixes a long-running issue most prominent on LG's G-series OLEDs… for the most part.

item.title: 'Not just generating images. It’s thinking' — ChatGPT Images 2.0 could fundamentally change how you make AI images
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0 upgrades image generation with better reasoning, clearer text, and more reliable outputs, bringing it closer to true multimodal AI

item.title: NASAs incredible new telescope will offer an atlas of the universe
item.description: NASA has finished building the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope for studying exoplanets, dark matter, and dark energy in the cosmos.

item.title: 'Pushpaganda is, at the highest level, a case of social engineering': Experts warn scammers are flooding Google Discover with AI-generated content spreading malicious notifications
item.description: Pushpaganda uses AI-generated content and deceptive tactics to trick users into enabling notifications that deliver scams and evade traditional security defenses.

item.title: Deloitte and Zoom cut parental leave and other benefits
item.description: The cuts could act as a precedent for other employers to reduce paid time off, parental leave and pensions, according to Business Insider. Starting next year, Deloitte and Zoom are cutting back on some of the most treasured employee benefits, Business Insider reports.

item.title: Apple’s new CEO is a hardware guy, but software is his biggest challenge
item.description: John Ternus’s leadership has already given us some of Apple’s best devices ever. But the company could use a reboot when it comes to software. When Tim Cook’s tenure as CEO of Apple was still young, tech-industry pundits obsessed over one aspect of his new gig above all others. After returning to the company he cofounded, Jobs presided over an incredible run of epoch-shifting products: the iMac, iPod, iTunes Music Store, iPhone, iPhone App Store, and iPad. If Cook didn’t extend that streak, conventional wisdom went, Apple’s glory days would be over....

item.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple: Here’s a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion
item.description: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.

item.title: Cash App now supports accounts for kids 6-12
item.description: Cash App, the banking and payments app run by Block, has added support for parent-managed kids accounts. The new accounts include key benefits from the service's normal account, with an eye towards teaching financial literacy to younger users ages 6 to 12. Cash App first allowed teenage users on its platform in 2021.As part of the "expanded Cash App Families experience," eligible legal guardians and parents can create managed accounts that offer "a dedicated place on the platform to send allowances, set aside savings, and track spending for their child, kickstarting their path to financial ind...

item.title: We translated the Palantir manifesto for actual human beings
item.description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp is a man in charge of one of the most important and frightening companies in the world. Karp's new book, cowritten with Nicholas Zamiska, is called The Technological Republic. After claiming "because we get asked a lot," Palantir posted a 22-point summary of the book that reads like a corporate manifesto. […]

item.title: YouTube is muting push notifications from channels you don't watch
item.description: YouTube notifications can get messy fast, particularly if you’re subscribed to a lot of different channels. To address that, today the company will begin muting push notifications from creators that you haven’t engaged with in the last month.The change to YouTube notifications began as a small trial the company tested out earlier this year. The idea behind it is that if a viewer continually receives notifications about content they don't engage with, this may eventually cause the user to disable YouTube notifications altogether. Now obviously, this is bad for YouTube. Turning off notifications...
